questions.q: CVE-2022-20828 required an attacker to have full administrative credentials on the Cisco FTD device before exploiting the CLI injection.
options: False — read-only or any authenticated CLI account was sufficient to exploit the restricted shell escape
True — only administrators could access the CLI commands vulnerable to injection

questions.q: Which shell metacharacter did attackers inject into Cisco FTD diagnostic CLI arguments to escape the restricted shell in CVE-2022-20828?
options: A semicolon (;), which terminated the legitimate CLI command and appended an arbitrary OS command
A dollar sign ($), which expanded environment variables and redirected output to a file

questions.q: When an attacker successfully exploited CVE-2022-20828, the injected OS commands executed with what privilege level?
options: A. As the limited 'ftd-service' account with no access to firewall configuration
B. As root (uid=0), because the underlying FTD CLI process ran with full OS privileges

questions.q: A compromised Cisco FTD firewall via CVE-2022-20828 gives an attacker the ability to modify live inspection policies, such as disabling IPS signatures.
options: True — root access on the FTD allows real-time manipulation of inspection policies, VPN keys, and pivot to the Firepower Management Center network
False — firewall inspection policies are stored off-device and cannot be changed from the OS shell

questions.q: What was Cisco's patch strategy for CVE-2022-20828, and what FTD version resolved the vulnerability?
options: A. Cisco issued a configuration workaround disabling the affected CLI commands; no firmware update was required
B. Cisco released FTD 7.0.2, which added strict allowlist-based validation of CLI argument characters to reject shell metacharacters

questions.q: CVE-2022-20773 required an attacker to first obtain a valid Cisco Umbrella user account before bypassing authentication.
options: False — the vulnerability was unauthenticated; an attacker could send a crafted SAML assertion with no valid credentials whatsoever
True — a low-privilege account was needed to intercept a valid SAML token before the XSW attack could proceed

questions.q: In the XML Signature Wrapping (XSW) attack used against Cisco Umbrella, what makes the forged SAML assertion pass the service provider's signature check?
options: A. The attacker obtains the IdP private signing key and generates a new valid signature over the forged assertion
B. The valid signature remains over the original legitimate assertion element; the SP reads the forged element first but validates the signature over the untouched original

questions.q: Cisco Umbrella's CVSS score for CVE-2022-20773 was 9.6, reflecting that any internet-accessible Umbrella SSO endpoint was exploitable with no authentication required.
options: True — no credentials were needed, and every internet-exposed Umbrella tenant SSO endpoint was at risk
False — the CVSS score was lower because exploitation required network adjacency, not internet access

questions.q: Why are SAML authentication bypasses particularly difficult to detect with traditional monitoring after a successful attack?
options: A. SAML traffic is always encrypted with a separate key that SIEM tools cannot inspect, making the forged assertion invisible in logs
B. The application receives what appears to be a valid, signed assertion from the trusted IdP, so the resulting session looks indistinguishable from a legitimate login

questions.q: The most reliable defense against XML Signature Wrapping attacks in SAML implementations is to use well-audited SAML libraries rather than custom XML parsing code.
options: True — established SAML libraries correctly enforce canonical form validation so the SP reads and verifies the same assertion element, preventing XSW restructuring
False — custom XML parsers are safer because they can be tailored to reject unexpected document structures that libraries might allow

questions.q: CVE-2019-1896 in the Cisco IMC REST API could only be exploited by an attacker who already had administrator-level IMC credentials.
options: False — any authenticated IMC account, including the default read-only monitoring account, was sufficient to exploit the remoteShare injection
True — the vulnerable virtual media endpoint required admin privilege to call, limiting exposure to privileged users

questions.q: Which REST API parameter in the Cisco IMC virtual media mount endpoint was vulnerable to OS command injection in CVE-2019-1896?
options: A. The 'Authorization' header value, where a crafted Bearer token triggered a format string vulnerability in the IMC parser
B. The 'remoteShare' JSON parameter, where a semicolon-injected value was passed directly to a shell command without sanitization

questions.q: Achieving root on the Cisco IMC via CVE-2019-1896 provides a level of persistence that survives a full operating system reinstall on the managed server.
options: True — the IMC is out-of-band management firmware separate from the server OS; root access enables firmware-level implants that persist through OS wipes
False — the IMC and server OS share the same storage, so reinstalling the OS also removes any attacker persistence from the IMC

questions.q: What is the recommended network-level mitigation for protecting Cisco UCS IMC interfaces against exploitation, in addition to patching firmware?
options: A. Expose the IMC management interface on the same VLAN as general user workstations so that monitoring tools have full visibility into IMC traffic
B. Restrict IMC management network access with strict ACLs, limiting connectivity to specific named management workstations only

questions.q: An attacker with root access on a Cisco UCS IMC can pivot to the UCS Manager management network, potentially gaining control over every server in the data center chassis.
options: True — the IMC connects to the UCS Manager fabric that manages all blades and servers in the chassis, making IMC compromise a data-center-wide threat
False — the IMC is isolated to a single server and has no network path to the UCS Manager or other servers in the chassis
